text.title: vui-market-context Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the vui-market-context methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Platform scores MUST cite 2025-2026 sources for install base, developer access status, and store policy. Pre-2024 data is forbidden as authoritative.

rationale.source: IDC 2025 voice assistant tracker; vendor 2025 earnings

rule.rationale: Developer access tightened sharply in 2024-2026; stale data produces wrong decisions.

rule.statement: Each platform candidate MUST be scored on developer_access (open / partial / restricted / closed). Restricted/closed platforms require explicit fallback path.

rationale.source: Alexa Skills Kit deprecation notices 2024; SiriKit limitations

rule.rationale: Many vendor platforms now restrict 3rd-party access to system intents; viability depends on this score.

rule.statement: Decision MUST name one primary platform AND ≥1 fallback. Single-platform lock-in MUST include a documented risk note.
rule.rationale: Voice platforms have shut down or limited 3rd-party access; fallback is risk parity.

rule.statement: Smart-home voice products MUST include Matter OR Home Assistant as a fallback for open distribution. Vendor-only smart-home distribution is forbidden as primary strategy.
rule.rationale: Vendor smart-home certifications are slow, expensive, and revocable; open paths are mandatory for survival.

rule.statement: Custom-LLM voice (OpenAI Realtime API, Gemini Live, Pipecat) IS a valid primary platform for app + IVR use cases. Vendor platforms MUST NOT be artificially preferred when custom-LLM fits better.

rationale.source: OpenAI Realtime API docs 2025; Pipecat docs 2025

rule.rationale: Custom-LLM voice now offers latency + flexibility competitive with vendor platforms for app-embedded voice.
